Personality Inventory for DSM-5 Scale Data

Source: R/data.R
pid_scales.Rd

Information about the scales (facets) in different versions of the PID-5, used by score_pid5() to map each scale to its item numbers.

Usage

pid_scales

Format

A named list of length 3 (elements FULL, SF, and BF), one per PID-5 version. Each element is a tibble with one row per scale and 5 columns:

Facet (named Domain in the BF element)

Name of the scale: the facet for the FULL and SF versions, the domain for the BF version

itemdata

A list column containing one item-data tibble per scale

nItems

The number of items in the scale

itemNumbers

A list column containing one item-number vector per scale

camelCase

The name of the scale converted to camel case (the score-output column stem)

Examples

pid_scales[["BF"]]
#> # A tibble: 5 × 5
#>   Domain               itemdata         nItems itemNumbers  camelCase          
#>   <chr>                <list>            <dbl> <named list> <chr>              
#> 1 Disinhibition        <tibble [5 × 3]>      5 <dbl [5]>    disinhibition      
#> 2 Detachment           <tibble [5 × 3]>      5 <dbl [5]>    detachment         
#> 3 Psychoticism         <tibble [5 × 3]>      5 <dbl [5]>    psychoticism       
#> 4 Negative affectivity <tibble [5 × 3]>      5 <dbl [5]>    negativeAffectivity
#> 5 Antagonism           <tibble [5 × 3]>      5 <dbl [5]>    antagonism
